memory: Project state is not a single mutable snapshot. The durable continuity model is a source-ordered ledger: canonical governance establishes the baseline; append-only queue updates contribute later observations; explicit reconciliation records may promote, supersede, or close those observations. Recency alone is insufficient: every effective-state claim must retain source identity, claim state, ownership, and a falsification route.

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is older than committed continuity queue updates and lists only completed M-001 for continuity_mining.
operations/queue-updates/M-RECONCILE-002-2026-07-14T0938-0400.json records M-RECONCILE-002 as active with three remaining actions.
CM-1021 already establishes that a stale canonical snapshot must not silently override newer source-bound progress.
inferred: The effective continuity queue currently includes M-RECONCILE-002 as active, while the canonical queue remains a governance baseline rather than a complete current-state representation.
A future reconciliation engine must order records by explicit semantic precedence, not file timestamp alone.
proposed: Use the precedence order canonical_baseline < append_only_observation < explicit_reconciliation for queue-state computation.
Reject any reconciliation that omits the exact source paths and immutable identities it incorporates.
Preserve superseded records rather than rewriting or deleting them.
superseded: Treating operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as the sole current queue when later committed queue updates exist.
Treating newest timestamp as sufficient proof of semantic authority.
unresolved: No executable reconciler currently computes effective queue state from all eligible queue-update records.
No authenticated exhaustive eligible-path enumeration has yet proved that every queue update was considered.
The originating sources for M-004, M-005, and M-006 remain unresolved.

precedence_model.meaning: Governance and initial queue state; remains authoritative for rules unless explicitly superseded.
Later source-bound progress or unresolved work; cannot erase the baseline.
A source-complete artifact that incorporates, supersedes, closes, or preserves earlier records with citations.

privacy_boundary: Public repository governance files and commit metadata only. Private chat, health information, credentials, identifying personal material, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.
falsification_route: Produce a source-complete reconciliation artifact or executable reconciler showing a different precedence rule, together with all eligible queue-update paths and immutable source identities. Any omitted eligible record or unbound source reopens the effective-state conclusion.
